summary: Close the final beta.3 UI/UX gauntlet finding by keeping narrow-viewer details in layout flow instead of overlaying the model bounds readout.

providedInputs.label: Owner-provided ScadMill functional specification v0.6 with amendments

decisions.decision: At widths through 599 pixels, place the viewer details panel in a dedicated grid row with static positioning instead of overlaying the model surface.
decisions.rationale: The wide-layout containment fix prevented the dimensions readout from covering details, but the narrow absolute overlay reversed the collision and could hide the dimensions. Separate rows make the two surfaces non-overlapping by construction.

testEvidence.criterion: The narrow viewer details panel cannot occlude the bounds readout.
testEvidence.test: Lock the 599-pixel breakpoint to a dedicated viewer-content row and static details positioning.

red.outcome: RED: 1 failed and 19 passed because the narrow details rule used position:absolute instead of the required in-flow layout. The exact observation is retained in the final UI/UX gate report.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx --reporter=default; pnpm.cmd exec biome lint src/ui/workbench.css tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx
green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file and 20 tests passed; Biome checked both changed files with no fixes.

nearMisses.sought: Apply the reported overlap correction at an assumed 720-pixel breakpoint.
nearMisses.confirmation: The stylesheet's actual compact-viewer transition is 599 pixels. The regression assertion and fix target that real rule rather than introducing a second breakpoint.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
